extraction.one_line_summary: KAMPENAERTS Victor won from two-rider sprint after repeated attacks with Riesebeek in final kilometers, dropped chase group after break fragmented on final circuit climb.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step Alpecin placed three riders in 15-man break, enabling Kampenaerts to attack repeatedly knowing Valter/Visenovsky could sit on.
Kampenaerts/Riesebeek repeatedly attacked each other solo in final 3km rather than collaborate, suggesting neither trusted sprint ability.
Peloton conceded 15-man break despite only 147km race and circuit format, revealing GC teams conserving for Stage 16 queen stage.

rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: negative: crashed out from 6th GC on supposedly straightforward stage, exposing Bora's queen stage vulnerability before critical mountain test.
rider_signals.MOLLEMA Bauke: negative: placed in 15-man break but couldn't match pace of weaker climbers on short 4th category circuit, chased 7 seconds down.
rider_signals.KAMPENAERTS Victor: positive: sustained repeated accelerations in final 3km while Riesebeek faded, showing superior punch despite time trial specialist profile.
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: neutral: Quick-Step placed him in break despite flat finale approaching, suggesting team prioritized numbers over specialist selection.
